# US Stock Watcher Next-Session Forecast — Monday 7/13

> **[Monday 7/13 session outlook] bullish lean — Strong expansion / bull · composite +37 · confidence 48%**

> **🔮 Next-session forecast** · The next U.S. regular session is Monday 7/13 (opens 9:30 AM ET / 22:30 KST 7/13).

_Data as of: 2026-07-11T05:56:06 · data quality: fresh_

## Executive summary

With markets closed for the weekend, this brief looks ahead to the next U.S. regular session, Monday 7/13, which opens at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST). Heading into that session, our composite market-state score stands at +37 out of 100, placing conditions firmly in "Strong expansion / bull" territory, though confidence in that read is moderate at 48%, based on four measured components. Six inputs — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning — remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of the composite, so the signal should be treated as a partial-information read rather than a full-spectrum diagnosis.

## Market diagnosis

**Indices.** Going into Monday's open, the major benchmarks enter from a modestly firm footing: the S&P 500 closed at 7,575.39, up 0.42% on the day; the Nasdaq-100 finished at 29,825.11, up 0.33%; the Dow settled at 52,637.01, up 0.29%; while small caps diverged, with the Russell 2000 slipping 0.49% to 2,977.81, underscoring a large-cap-led tone as the new week approaches.

**Market drivers.** Three of the four tracked drivers lean supportive of the bullish setup — volatility, index trend, and the yield curve are all flagged as tailwinds — while the US dollar stands out as the lone headwind among the group.

**Breadth & concentration.** The overall market-state score of +37/100 corresponds to "Strong expansion / bull," carried with 48% confidence across the four components that could be measured. As noted above, Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning were not captured in this read and have been reweighted out of the composite.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership is concentrated in Financials, Health Care, and Industrials, while Consumer Staples, Real Estate, and Energy are lagging — a rotation pattern consistent with a risk-on, cyclically-tilted backdrop heading into the session.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.54%, with the dollar index (DXY) at 100.97, up a slight 0.03% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude eased 0.93% to $71.41, and gold fell 0.64% to $4,104.10.

## Outlook

**Short term.** Framed specifically for the Monday 7/13 session, the near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the "Strong expansion / bull" market state and its 48% confidence reading. This view would flip should the overall composite score cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance carries the same constructive bias, again anchored to the "Strong expansion / bull" classification at 48% confidence, with the same conditioning caveat: a move of the overall score below zero would reverse this lean.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending further out, the medium-to-long-term outlook likewise remains constructive under the current "Strong expansion / bull" read and 48% confidence level, subject to the identical flip condition — a crossing of the overall score below the zero threshold.

## Top news

Coverage feeding into this outlook includes: "Higher Treasury Yields Are Driving Interest In These Regional Bank Stocks" (simplywall.st); "US Government Sold $743 Billion of Treasury Securities this Week, 30-Year Yield at 5.06% on Inflation, Lax Fed, Supply Fears" (Wolf Street); "Market Sets Up, 5 Stocks Near Buy Points; Big Earnings Ahead" (Investor's Business Daily); "SK chief dismisses peak fears, pledges massive AI push after Nasdaq debut" (Korea JoongAng Daily); "Analysts still expect US Treasury yields to fall despite inflation" (IDNFinancials.com); and "S&P 500 rises before earnings as SK Hynix debuts" (The Business Times).
